title: Pre-code contract freeze and implementation slicing
releaseGate: Do not publish before the user confirms the frontend workbench shape, the first code slice boundary, the new program root implementation layout, and the initial OpenClaw task split.

stories.title: Freeze MVP frontend workbench information architecture
stories.scope: Normalize the first-phase frontend workbench definition into one durable discussion pack covering Program 首页, Program 工作台, 文档阅读面板, Batch 审核面板, navigation mode, readiness view, and the exact boundary of `只读 + 审阅 + 状态推进`.
stories.nonGoals: Do not start frontend implementation, do not add model/profile management to the first batch, and do not decide visual style details that belong to the later frontend design discussion.
acceptance: Frontend workbench pages, panels, states, and unresolved decisions are written in durable docs.
The pack clearly states whether batch-001 code scope excludes `AI 优化` and `轻量编辑`.
The pack can be used directly in the next human frontend discussion without needing conversation memory.
validation: rg -n "Program 工作台|Batch 审核面板|只读 \+ 审阅 \+ 状态推进" /Users/huangliang/project/OneOPS-ALL/docs/prd-autodev/generic-ai-autodev-platform /Users/huangliang/project/OneOPS-ALL/docs/development/generic-ai-autodev-platform
notes: This is the final planning normalization story before frontend implementation slicing.

stories.title: Freeze planning, control, and execution boundary contracts
stories.scope: Consolidate the durable boundary between planning plane, control plane, and `dagengine` execution kernel, including candidate lane responsibility, batch readiness gate, and the minimum contract that must exist before any OpenClaw task is created.
stories.nonGoals: Do not create runtime loop configs, do not publish stories, and do not turn candidate objects into code schemas yet.
acceptance: Planning/control/execution responsibilities are explicit and non-overlapping.
Candidate lane usage and publish-ready gate are documented in a way that can be reviewed before code.
The docs state exactly why `targetTaskIds` remain empty in this draft batch.

stories.title: Normalize template rollout and pre-code gate documents
stories.scope: Make sure the `01-07` candidate standard documents, template rollout mapping, review note, and final-readiness note are internally consistent and ready for the pre-code human alignment session.
stories.nonGoals: Do not expand into `08-10`, and do not add implementation assumptions that the current docs cannot support.
acceptance: `template-rollout-mapping.md`, `review.md`, and `evidence/final-readiness.md` no longer contain planning placeholders.
Residual risks and required human confirmations are explicit.

evidencePolicy.notes: This batch is a pre-code planning batch. Evidence means durable planning artifacts, not runtime execution output.

stopConditions: frontend workbench shape is still ambiguous after the next human discussion
the first code slice still mixes read-only work with AI optimization or full editing without explicit approval
the implementation root and validation baseline for generic-ai-autodev remain undefined
someone tries to create or enable OpenClaw tasks before the draft batch is reviewed
